title: Regime-aware CV and experts: leave one race-year out, then blend slick/wet and green-flag/caution models
summary: The dataset mixes years, circuits, wet compounds, and even pre-season testing, so random or weak grouping can overstate CV by reusing race-specific distributions.
rationale: The dataset mixes years, circuits, wet compounds, and even pre-season testing, so random or weak grouping can overstate CV by reusing race-specific distributions. GroupKFold with race-year groups is the minimum safeguard, and separate experts often help when behavior changes sharply across regimes.
implementation_hint: Validate with `Race_Year` or stricter leave-one-event-out groups, then train specialist models for slick vs wet compounds and normal-green vs caution/rain regimes. Use a simple gating rule from compound/track-status/weather to blend expert probabilities.
expected_effect: Better offline reliability and potential leaderboard lift if current CV is inflated by event leakage or if one global model is averaging across incompatible regimes.
risk: If current CV is already strict, gains may be small; splitting into experts can reduce sample size and overfit rare wet/testing regimes.
